title: Building TraceMap With Codex, Kiro, and Qodo
h1: Building TraceMap with Codex, Kiro, and Qodo
description: How TraceMap is built with Codex worktrees, Kiro specs, implementation-state notes, PR review loops, and Qodo feedback while keeping the core scanner deterministic.
ogDescription: TraceMap uses specs, worktrees, review loops, and PR feedback to keep deterministic analysis work small, auditable, and easy to resume.
cardDescription: How specs, worktrees, review loops, and PR feedback keep a deterministic analysis project reviewable and resumable.
hero: TraceMap is a deterministic analysis tool, but the project still benefits from a workflow that makes intent, implementation state, and review feedback visible to humans and agents.
calloutHtml: Read why TraceMap exists or inspect the example evidence packets.

title: Why TraceMap Exists
description: Why TraceMap exists: dependency and contract-impact review should start from deterministic evidence instead of manual indexing, memory, or broad search.
ogDescription: TraceMap was built to turn manual dependency indexing and contract-impact review into deterministic static evidence.
cardDescription: How dependency and contract-impact review turns into manual indexing work, and why TraceMap makes that work evidence-backed.
hero: TraceMap started from a practical review problem: teams need to understand unfamiliar systems, contract changes, and dependency surfaces without turning every investigation into hand-built maps.
calloutHeading: Want to see the shape of the evidence?
calloutHtml: Review the scan packet example or read what TraceMap solves for engineering teams.

title: What TraceMap Solves for Engineering Teams
h1: What TraceMap solves for engineering teams
description: What TraceMap solves for engineering teams: less manual indexing, better handoff, auditable review evidence, and coverage-aware decisions.
ogDescription: TraceMap turns dependency and contract-impact questions into auditable static evidence packets.
cardDescription: Why manual dependency indexing does not scale, and how static evidence packets make review work easier to hand off and revisit.
hero: TraceMap exists for the moments when a team needs to understand dependency and contract-impact risk across code that is too large, too unfamiliar, or too fragmented for manual indexing to be reliable.
calloutHeading: Want to inspect the artifacts first?
calloutHtml: Start with the examples, then run the public demo against checked-in samples.
